stories.title: With strikes above and crackdowns on the ground, Iranians describe life under siege
stories.description: A state-imposed internet blackout has obscured the reality of life in Iran as the war rages on. Those fleeing through neighboring countries share a rare glimpse of what life is like in Iran.

stories.title: Author Scott Anderson on Iran's power structure after death of top security official
stories.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with journalist and author Scott Anderson about Iran's power structure after the killing of Ali Larijani, the head of Iran's Supreme National Security Council.

stories.title: Iran retaliates after Israel kills 2 top Iranian officials
stories.description: Iran launched a barrage of missiles after Israel killed two top Iranian officials, including Ali Larijani, the head of Iran's Supreme National Security Council. Israel also struck central Beirut.

stories.title: Cheap drones are reshaping modern warfare — and catching the U.S. off guard
stories.description: As Operation Epic Fury enters its third week, relentless attacks by cheap Iranian drones are being fended off by multi-million-dollar U.S. interceptors. How long can the math hold up?

stories.title: Morning news brief
stories.description: Iranians fleeing their country share rare glimpse of what life is like amid war, Trump floats idea of "taking" Cuba, the Federal Reserve is expected to hold its benchmark interest rate steady.

stories.title: Once the airstrikes stop, Iran's nuclear threat leaves no easy endgame
stories.description: Even after airstrikes end, Iran's nuclear threat looms and diplomacy may be too late.

stories.title: War with Iran strands about 20,000 seafarers in the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Jacqueline Smith of the International Transport Workers' Federation about the roughly 20,000 seafarers stuck in the Strait of Hormuz in the midst of the Iran war.

stories.title: Surge in US gas prices deepens political peril for Trump over Iran
stories.description: The odds of a quick resolution to the war appear to be dwindling by the day, and the political stakes for the president are only growing.

stories.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
stories.description: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

stories.title: Israel says 'limited' ground operations under way in Lebanon
stories.description: Troops are targeting "Hezbollah strongholds" in south Lebanon, where strikes reportedly killed nine people on Monday.

stories.title: How might the UK support shipping in the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Ben Chu from BBC Verify has been looking at what role the Royal Navy could play in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Two charged in death of anti-Iranian regime activist in Canada
stories.description: Police say the motive remains under investigation but noted the three had disputes on social media.

stories.title: Who wants what from the Iran war?
stories.description: Most people, although not everybody, want this war to end as quickly as possible. But on what terms?

stories.title: Iran taking steps to prevent anti-establishment protests, Tehran residents tell BBC
stories.description: Residents describe new security checkpoints around the capital, where they say people are stopped and searched.

stories.title: Iran hits key UAE oil port and Dubai airport
stories.description: The port of Fujairah plays a crucial role in helping keep global supplies moving when the Strait of Hormuz is blocked.

stories.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
stories.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.

stories.title: Iranian footballer says 'everything will be fine' as she trains with Australian team
stories.description: Several players were granted Australian visas after concerns the team would face repercussions at home.

stories.title: Iran captain latest footballer to drop asylum bid, Iranian state media says
stories.description: Five of the seven members of the women's football delegation who originally sought to stay in Australia have now chosen to return home.

stories.title: US names six crew killed in refuelling plane crash in Iraq
stories.description: The aircraft was on a combat mission as part of ongoing US operations against Iran when it crashed in western Iraq on Thursday.

stories.title: BBC visits aftermath of Israeli strike on Lebanon that killed family as IDF targets Hezbollah
stories.description: A relative tells BBC those killed were civilians and not Hezbollah operatives, but the Israel Defense Forces says it was targeting "terrorist infrastructure".

stories.title: Why has the US targeted Iran's Kharg Island?
stories.description: The tiny island is home to one of the most critical pieces of Iran's energy infrastructure.

stories.title: Hamas urges key ally Iran to halt attacks on Gulf states
stories.description: The Palestinian group also affirms Tehran's right to defend itself from "aggression" by the US and Israel.

stories.title: BTS fever grips Seoul as boy band return after four-year hiatus
stories.description: The long-awaited return to the stage of K-pop sensation BTS is set to boost South Korea's tourism and consumer economy. Our regional correspondents report. But first: the war in Iran upends the spring travel season for airlines and passengers alike, and investors await the latest...

stories.title: Morocco taking AFCON title from Senegal dubbed 'joke of the century'
stories.description: PRESS REVIEW – Wednesday, March 18, 2026: The Africa Cup of Nations has seen another unbelievable turn as the Confederation of African Football (CAF) has stripped Senegal of their 2025 African Champions title, to give it to Morocco. Also, the Guardian reveals that the UK took par...

stories.title: Top US official resigns over Trump's war, says Iran posed no imminent threat
stories.description: Joe Kent, the director of the National Counterterrorism Center, announced his resignation on Tuesday, citing his concerns about the justification for military strikes in Iran and saying he “cannot in good conscience” back the Trump administration’s war. “Iran posed no imminent th...

stories.title: Iran to hold funeral for slain security chief as it vows vengeance
stories.description: Iran will hold a funeral Wednesday for its security chief Ali Larijani as it vowed revenge, firing off a wave of missiles at Israel after it killed the powerful figure in an air strike. A barrage of Iranian missiles killed two people near Israel's commercial hub Tel Aviv, while G...

stories.title: Iran missile fire kills two in central Israel
stories.description: An Iranian missile barrage killed two people near Israel's commercial hub Tel Aviv, medics said Wednesday, with the national railway company announcing shrapnel impact disrupted train services. Authorities reported that falling munitions had hit multiple sites in central Israel i...

stories.title: On the ground: Lebanon says Israel struck without warning, killing at least 10 in Beirut
stories.description: Israel stepped up airstrikes in central Beirut on Wednesday, killing at least 10 people and destroying a 10-storey building, Lebanese authorities said, in attacks that shook the capital in the third week of Israel's war with Hezbollah. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin reports from the...

stories.title: Two key figures of Iranian leadership killed: Who's next?
stories.description: Ali Larijani was the head of Iran's Security Council and a key voice in the ear of the late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Gholamreza Soleimani was the head of the Basij militia. Both were pillars of Iran's security apparatus. If they have indeed been taken out, the question is who repl...

stories.title: Starmer says 'focus must remain on Ukraine' despite war in Iran after talks with Zelensky
stories.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer told Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky that international attention must remain on Ukraine's fight against Russia despite the US-Israeli war with Iran. The two leaders met in London and signed a new defence partnership focused on counterin...

stories.title: Live: Iranian intelligence minister Khatib killed overnight, says Israel
stories.description: Iranian intelligence ​minister ‌Esmail Khatib ⁠was "eliminated" in ‌a strike overnight, ⁠Israel's ⁠Defence ​Minister Israel Katz ​said on Wednesday. Iran has not yet confirmed ‌Khatib's reported death. The news comes after Israeli strikes on central Beirut – which came without wa...

stories.title: Iran using cluster munitions in retaliatory strikes on Israel
stories.description: Iran is continuing its retaliatory strikes against Israel, with some damage to homes in central Israel this Tuesday. Israel says Tehran is using cluster munitions, which explode mid-air and indiscriminately scatter bomblets across a wide area. FRANCE 24's Carys Garland reports.

stories.title: Top Iranian officials killed in Israeli airstrike in wartime blow to country's leadership
stories.description: Iran has confirmed the deaths of two senior security officials, considered among the most powerful figures in the country, in overnight strikes: Ali Larijani, secretary of Iran’s Supreme National Security Council, and General Gholamreza Soleimani, head of the Revolutionary Guard’...

stories.title: Iran confirms security chief Ali Larijani killed, state media says
War in Iran exposes cracks in Trump's MAGA coalition
stories.description: Joe Kent, the director of the National Counterterrorism Center, announced his resignation on Tuesday, citing his concerns about the justification for military strikes in Iran and saying he “cannot in good conscience” back the Trump administration’s war. Kent, a former Green Beret...

stories.title: War on Iran: As top leaders and commanders are killed, 'regime will harden and close ranks'
stories.description: As Israel announces it has killed Iran's security chief, Ali Larijani, Annette Young is joined by Negah Angha, Visiting Fellow at King's College London. In assessing the reported killing of senior Iranian leadership figures, Ms. Angha argues that such developments represent a ser...

stories.title: Larijani was 'key to Iran's reach in the Gulf region'
stories.description: Reporting from Doha for FRANCE 24, Hoda Abdel-Hamid explains that Iranian security chief Ali Larijani "was key to Iran's reach to the Gulf region" adding that "the GCC and Iran were two parties deeply suspicious of each other, it took decades for them to get close to each other a...

stories.title: How Iran defied Trump threats to emerge as Strait of Hormuz gatekeeper
stories.description: Trump has called on other navies to help open the strait, but countries are instead striking deals with Iran.

stories.title: Israel says it killed Iran intel minister in third assassination in 2 days
stories.description: If the death of Esmaeil Khatib is confirmed, it would be the third major high-profile assassination in successive days.

stories.title: Iranian diaspora celebrates Persian festival wrapped in US, Israeli flags
stories.description: Dancing on a beach in Barcelona, members of the Iranian diaspora celebrated the Persian festival of Chaharshanbe Suri.

stories.title: ‘US and Israel have taken majority of Iran’s ballistic missile capability’
stories.description: ‘US and Israel have taken the vast majority of Iran’s ballistic missile capability’

stories.title: Why Israel targets Beirut’s Dahiyeh and what the suburb means to Lebanon
stories.description: Dahiyeh's story is not only about Hezbollah or Israel. It is about how it came to embody Lebanon’s deepest inequalities.

stories.title: The oil logic behind Trump’s war on Iran
stories.description: The Kharg Island strike shows how US force is calibrated to avoid disrupting global oil flows.

stories.title: Aftermath of Iranian ‘revenge’ strikes on Israel, two killed
stories.description: Widespread damage was left across areas near Tel Aviv after overnight Iranian missile strikes.

stories.title: Israel bombs central Beirut, strafes south, east Lebanon, killing over 20
stories.description: Wave of Israeli air attacks launched as ground offensive widens in south where Hezbollah is fighting Israeli forces.

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 19 of US-Israel attacks?
stories.description: Iran vows revenge after Israeli strikes kill security chief Ali Larijani and Basij commander Gholamreza Soleimani.

stories.title: Iran launches ‘revenge’ missile attack on Israel after assassinations
stories.description: Iran continues to face a daily onslaught of US and Israeli air attacks in Tehran and across the country.

stories.title: Iran fires missiles, drones across Gulf, region remains in war crosshairs
stories.description: Qatar, UAE, Kuwait and Saudi Arabia report new blasts, interceptions, with war edging to 3-week mark.

stories.title: Iran vows revenge for killing of security chief Ali Larijani by Israel
stories.description: Iran vowed on Wednesday a “decisive” retaliation for the death of its security chief Ali Larijani, firing off a wave of missiles at Israel which said it killed him in an air strike. A barrage of Iranian missiles killed two people near Israel’s commercial hub Tel Aviv, while Gulf ...

stories.title: PIA suspends flights to UAE’s Fujairah for 2 days in view of ‘security situation’
stories.description: The Pakistan International Airlines (PIA) on Wednesday morning suspended its flights to Fujairah in the United Arab Emirates (UAE) for the next 48 hours in view of the “security situation” as the Middle East war persists. In a statement, the carrier’s spokesperson said that for t...

stories.title: Dar to depart on Riyadh visit for meeting of regional FMs, will call for ‘swift end’ to Middle East conflict
stories.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar will depart for Riyadh on Wednesday for a meeting with the foreign ministers of regional countries, where he will advocate for a “swift end” to the conflict in the Middle East, the Foreign Office (FO) said. The conflict began w...

stories.title: UN says 36,000 Palestinians displaced in occupied West Bank in one year
stories.description: GENEVA: The United Nations urged Israel on Tuesday to halt immediately its dramatic settlement expansion in the occupied West Bank, raising concerns of “ethnic cleansing” with over 36,000 Palestinians displaced in a single year. A fresh report from the United Nations rights offic...

stories.title: China says delay in Trump visit not linked to Hormuz closure
stories.description: BEIJING: China said on Tuesday it had “noted” clarifications from the United States about the reasons for a possible delay to President Donald Trump’s planned trip. Trump expected to visit Beijing at the end of March, but said on Monday he had asked Beijing to delay his summit wi...

stories.title: Kyiv deploys over 200 anti-drone experts in ME
stories.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky and Nato Secretary General Mark Rutte meet at 10 Downing Street.—Reuters LONDON: President Volodymyr Zelensky said on Tuesday that 201 Ukrainian anti-drone military experts were in the Middle East to help...

stories.title: Iran’s national security chief Ali Larijani, Basij commander Soleimani assassinated in US-Israeli strikes
stories.description: Iran’s national security chief Ali Larijani and the commander of paramilitary force Basij, Gholamreza Soleimani, were assassinated in US-Israeli strikes on Tuesday. The confirmation of Larijani’s assassination came hours after Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz said he had been...

stories.title: Trump blasts ‘foolish’ Nato on Iran, says US needs no help after allies rebuff call for help on Hormuz
stories.description: US President Donald Trump lashed out Tuesday at “foolish” Nato over Iran, saying the United States needs no help after allies rebuffed his calls to join efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Trump said most US allies had rejected his push to escort ships through the crucial wat...

stories.title: ‘Iran posed no imminent threat to our nation’: Senior US counter-terrorism official resigns to protest Mideast war
stories.description: A senior US counter-terrorism official resigned on Tuesday to protest the US-Israeli war against Iran and said the Islamic Republic posed no imminent threat to the United States. “I cannot in good conscience support the ongoing war in Iran,” Joseph Kent, the director of the Natio...

stories.title: Iran’s uranium enrichment secures independence, peaceful atom — supreme leader
stories.description: Ali Khamenei added that the US and other countries are not interested in Iran's progress and have put forward conditions presupposing a complete halt of uranium enrichment on Iranian territory

stories.title: Tehran could weigh nuclear deal with US with Iran-based enrichment consortium — Axios
stories.description: According to the report, a sixth round of talks on the Iranian nuclear program between the US president’s Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iran’s top diplomat, Abbas Araghchi, could be held this weekend in a Middle Eastern country

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
